evidence_basis_summary: Paper takes POPQUORN politeness and offensiveness subsets as given corpora (§3.4) with no human work selecting which materials belong in the analysis universe.
notes: Upgraded from low: full text confirms corpus is pre-selected benchmark data.

evidence_basis_summary: Paper is a statistical-inference method (IPW/PPI-style correction) and makes no causal-mechanism claims requiring human discipline (§3.2).
notes: Upgraded from low: full text confirms no causal-mechanism work.

evidence_basis_summary: Paper's central move is reframing the estimand from a scalar ground truth to a vector over demographic perspectives (§1, §3.1), which is precisely the boundary/perspective decision and pressures splitting into boundary vs. perspective components.
notes: Upgraded from low: abstract and §3.1 make the split-pressure reading explicit.

evidence_basis_summary: Pluralism in the paper operates over annotator perspectives, not over multiple corpora; only POPQUORN politeness and offensiveness subsets plus synthetic data are used (§3.4).
notes: Upgraded from low: full text confirms single-source corpus with no pluralist-of-sources argument.

evidence_basis_summary: PDI is itself a protocol for allocating a human annotation budget across burn-in and adaptive batches with IPW correction and bootstrap CIs (§3.2), but the paper does not address logging, versioning, or disclosure, hence partial and split pressure between workflow-design and provenance components.
notes: Upgraded from low: Methods §3.2 supports workflow-design component; provenance/disclosure not discussed.

evidence_basis_summary: Discussion §5 gestures at generalizing PDI to RLHF, LLM-as-judge, and survey simulation but frames this as future work, not as human transportability adjudication within the case.
notes: Upgraded from low: full text shows transportability is mentioned only as extension, not performed.

evidence_basis_summary: Annotations come from Prolific crowd workers rated on scales (§3.4); no fieldwork, interview, or interpretive-setting work is present.
notes: Upgraded from low: full text confirms crowdsourced rating design.

evidence_basis_summary: Paper allocates human annotation budget across demographic groups (§3.2, Tables 2 and 4) but this is statistical sample allocation, not authorship/disclosure/accountability assignment.
notes: Upgraded from low: full text confirms no authorship/accountability framing; labor allocation is purely sampling.

evidence_basis_summary: Humans define the rating constructs (politeness 1-5, offensiveness 1-5) and the demographic stratification axes (gender/age/education) used for the estimand (§3.1, §3.4); these boundary decisions are necessary for validity.
notes: Upgraded from low: §3.1 and §3.4 are explicit about human-set categories and scales.

evidence_basis_summary: PDI's core contribution is adaptive human sampling concentrated on demographic groups where LLM proxies are least reliable, explicitly to preserve subgroup disagreement rather than collapse to majority vote (Abstract, §1, §3.2).
notes: Upgraded from low: paper is directly about preserving this role.

evidence_basis_summary: No counterfactual or cross-population transportability claims are adjudicated; the paper stays within POPQUORN and synthetic simulations (§3.4, §4.3).
notes: Upgraded from low: full text confirms absence.

evidence_basis_summary: IPW/PPI rectified estimator (§3.2) calibrates LLM proxy against human labels, but this is statistical correction to a held-out human estimand, not benchmark/simulator calibration against an external reference distribution.
notes: Upgraded from low: role label interpreted narrowly per codebook; notes that PPI-style rectification is adjacent but not a match.

evidence_basis_summary: Annotation is remote Prolific rating with no fieldwork or interactive interview; humans do not shape what respondents disclose (§3.4).
notes: Upgraded from low: full text confirms crowdsourced design.

evidence_basis_summary: No archival, tacit, or contextual interpretive work is invoked; demographics enter only as features for the error-predictor (§3.2).
notes: Upgraded from low: full text confirms absence.

evidence_basis_summary: No title/abstract triage, study screening, or early ranking task is present; the pipeline starts from already-selected text instances (§3.1).
notes: Upgraded from low: full text confirms not applicable.

role_label: Routine coder on high-consensus, ground-truth-rich tasks
evidence_basis_summary: Paper explicitly argues ground truth does NOT exist for subjective tasks (§1) and that collapsing disagreement is invalid; AI substitution for routine coding is precisely what is contested, so the cluster applies only in inverted form.
notes: Upgraded from low: full text clarifies paper's stance - this role-cluster's premise (ground truth available) is rejected for the case's tasks.

evidence_basis_summary: Paper concerns annotation of existing text, not AI-assisted prose drafting or polishing; no role for AI as a writer appears anywhere in the pipeline.
notes: Upgraded from low: full text confirms not applicable.
